title: Police officer arrested for hit-and-run
bodyText:
Breaking News
                            Update

GEORGE NEWS - The police officer involved in a hit-and-run incident in Nelson Mandela Boulevard on 29 July in which a boy (5) died and another young child was injured, was arrested earlier today, 16 August.


> The warrant officer stationed at the Thembalethu Detective branch was arrested on charges of culpable homicide and several other traffic related offences which include failing to stop at the scene of an accident and determining any injuries and failing to report an accident.


He remains in custody and will appear in court on Monday, 19 August.

It is alleged that the young children, who were crossing Nelson Mandela Boulevard, were struck by a white Nissan Hardbody of the Thembalethu Detective branch that was driven by the warrant officer.

It is further alleged that the officer, who was on duty at the time, left the scene without reporting the accident.

Lt.Col. Malcolm Pojie, the provincial police spokesperson, earlier today said they were informed that the member had been arrested by members of the Independent Police Investigative Directorate (Ipid) this morning.


> "Meanwhile a departmental investigation is underway with regards to the conduct of the member," said Pojie.


George Herald is awaiting comment from Ipid.

Previous article: Leads in fatal hit-and-run point to police vehicle

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
